methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Bedrock Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Boris Sofman, currently valued at $1.75B post-money on $270M total raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company in the sector, with peer median valuation of $500M across five same-stage robotics firms (1X Technologies at $10.0B, Dexterity at $1.6B, Collaborative Robotics at $500M, Fairmat at $350M, and GrayMatter Robotics at $300M).

Two methods were applied to triangulate fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from the $1.75B Series B post-money valuation, implying $3.3B at the next funding stage. Peer-median analysis across five comparable Series B robotics companies yields a $500M implied valuation, reflecting the wide dispersion in the peer set (range: $300M–$10.0B). The peer method pulls downward, likely reflecting valuation clustering below Bedrock's current mark.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.8B (high), with a median of $1.9B. This places Bedrock's current $1.75B valuation near the median, consistent with stage and sector positioning, though the wide peer range ($300M–$10.0B) reflects substantial heterogeneity in robotics valuations and underscores uncertainty around comparable company selection in this nascent sector.
